heading: Rules and regulations; employees
label: 1547 Rules and regulations; employees
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to , Conservation.section 18c of Title 16Provisions of par. (b) authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to employ “without regard to the civil-service laws” were omitted as such employment is subject to the civil service laws unless specifically excepted by those laws or by laws enacted subsequent to Executive Order 8743, , issued by the President pursuant to the , , which covered most excepted positions into the classified (competitive) civil service. The Order is set out as a note under , Government Organization and Employees.Apr. 23, 1941Act of Nov. 26, 1940, ch. 919, title I, § 154 Stat. 1211section 3301 of Title 5“Chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5” substituted in par. (b) for “the Classification Act of 1949, as amended” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 5.Pub. L. 89–554, § 7(b)80 Stat. 631Sept. 6, 1966
Amendments1949—Par. (b). Act , substituted “Classification Act of 1949” for “Classification Act of 1923”.Oct. 28, 1949

children.label: a
children.text: To prescribe, amend, and repeal such rules and regulations as he may deem necessary, and to accept contributions for carrying out the purposes of said sections; and

children.label: b
children.text: To employ, subject to chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5, one special assistant and not to exceed five artists and illustrators.
